tweets.text:
Timing is everything:

TBH launched when teens were tired of Instagram perfection.
Gas launched when teens wanted positivity after TikTok toxicity.

Same product, different moments. Read the room.

$700B annual remittances.

$44B lost to fees every year.

That's the size of the market stablecoins are eating. Not theoretical. Already happening.

CBDC privacy paradox:

• Users want: Anonymous cash-like payments
• Governments want: Traceable for AML/Tax
• Current solution: Surveillance with limits
• Result: Neither side fully happy

The tension is the feature, not the bug.

DeFi lending rates vs TradFi:

• US 10Y Treasury: ~4.2%
• Aave USDC: ~3-8% (variable)
• Compound: Similar range

The spread isn't magic. It's risk premium for smart contract exposure.

Soulbound tokens (SBTs) - the idea:

• Non-transferable NFTs
• Represent reputation/credentials
• Can't be sold, only earned

The reality: Still theoretical. No major adoption yet.

Good concept, needs time.

Bitcoin ETF inflows: $10B+ in first month.

This isn't retail FOMO. This is institutions finally getting permission to allocate.

The wall of money has a process. It's just starting.

Why Gas worked when other compliment apps failed:

1. Waited 2 years (post-COVID timing)
2. Focused on high schools (dense networks)
3. Limited daily polls (scarcity = engagement)
4. No anonymous messaging (safer)

Execution details matter.

Stablecoins in cross-border trade:

• Importer in Nigeria
• Exporter in China
• Banks: 5-7 days, high fees
• USDT: 10 minutes, <1%

Not just remittances. B2B trade is next.

Product lesson from 14 failed apps:

Users don't want your app.

They want the feeling your app gives them.

TBH = feeling seen
Gas = feeling popular

Build the feeling, not the features.

India's digital rupee:

• 1M+ retail transactions daily
• Main use: merchant payments
• Integration: UPI (existing rails)
• Strategy: Gradual, not revolutionary

Sometimes riding existing rails beats building new ones.
